basics.name: Yulun Wu
basics.label: Data Scientist
basics.summary: Data Scientist with a strong mathematical foundation and hands-on experience in machine learning, predictive analytics, and AI-driven solutions. Skilled in building end-to-end data pipelines, deploying large language models, and translating complex data into actionable business insights.

work.name: Midea Group Co., Ltd.
work.summary: Developed AI-powered systems for industrial energy optimization, intelligent inspection, and fire-hazard detection.
highlights: Developed a micro-grid scheduling agent using mixed-integer programming to optimize real-time battery charging/discharging, lowering plant electricity costs by 12.5% while processing 15,000+ data points daily.

Designed an intelligent substation-inspection report generator fusing multi-modal anomaly-detection algorithms with a knowledge graph and LLM reasoning, boosting report throughput 15x (42 s/report) and achieving 98.5% defect-identification accuracy.

Co-built an industrial-scale fire-hazard detection system; applied LoRA fine-tuning to the Qwen-VL vision-language model, reaching >92% image-hazard detection accuracy with 800 ms response time and established an automated data closed-loop training-to-deployment pipeline.

work.name: Wharton AI & Analytics Initiative
work.summary: Led the Wharton AI & Analytics Accelerator Team to develop AI-driven sales forecasting and business intelligence tools.
highlights: Led the team to develop an AI-driven tool that predicted sales trends for Spencer's Gifts, improving forecast accuracy by 25% and boosting sales efficiency by 18%.
Designed and implemented predictive models (Prophet, XGBoost) in Python achieving 91.2% accuracy, delivering actionable insights through interactive Power BI dashboards used by 500+ stakeholders.

Managed large-scale data pipelines on AWS and Databricks, utilizing SQL and PySpark to incorporate diverse datasets including marketing campaigns and social media analytics, enhancing forecast accuracy by 15%.

work.name: Novo Nordisk (China) Pharmaceuticals Co., Ltd.
work.summary: Led database migration and built predictive analytics for pharmaceutical sales optimization.
highlights: Collaborated with Microsoft China to lead a company-wide SQL database migration project, consolidating five disparate databases into a unified system. Optimized SQL queries reducing runtime by 30%.

Leveraged Python and statistical techniques to process and analyze historical sales promotion data. Generated predictive insights using SHAP interpretability that improved promotional budget allocation accuracy by 20%, contributing to $100,000 in cost savings.

work.name: Central Institute of Mental Health
work.summary: Conducted statistical analysis on neurophysiological activity data combining fMRI and heart rate signals.
highlights: Utilized Python and MATLAB for data preprocessing, GLM modeling, and trend identification to uncover relationships between brain activity and stress responses.
Collaborated with a multidisciplinary team to deliver a comprehensive presentation at an academic seminar at Heidelberg University, engaging over 20 scholars and researchers.

projects.name: AI-Driven Sales Forecasting (Spencer's Gifts)
projects.summary: Led the Wharton AI & Analytics Accelerator Team to build predictive models (Prophet, XGBoost) and data pipelines on AWS/Databricks, achieving 91.2% forecast accuracy and boosting sales efficiency by 18%.

projects.name: Industrial AI Platform (Midea Group)
projects.summary: Developed micro-grid scheduling agents, intelligent inspection report generators with LLM reasoning, and LoRA-fine-tuned vision-language models for fire-hazard detection across industrial facilities.

projects.name: Customer Churn Prediction
projects.summary: Built predictive models (Logistic Regression, Random Forest, XGBoost) on 7,000+ customer records with SMOTE balancing, achieving 90% accuracy and identifying strategies for 15% churn reduction.

projects.name: Pharmaceutical Sales Analytics (Novo Nordisk)
projects.summary: Led SQL database migration consolidating 5 databases, optimized queries reducing runtime by 30%, and built predictive models improving promotional budget allocation by 20%.
